Assembly: The assembly csSphPalu1.1 is based on 30x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The genome is tetraploid so this assembly represents one copy of each chromosome from each sub genome. This includes a single copy of a putative sex chromosome and multiple copies of a michrochromosome which appears to be underrepresented based on read coverage.

Assembly: The assembly fTraTra1.2 is based on 105x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Falcon-unzip, retained haplotig identification with purge_dups, 10X based scaffolding with scaff10x, Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2, Arrow polishing, and two rounds of FreeBayes polishing. The mitochondrial assembly was produced at The Rockefeller University using mitoVGP. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds are named by synteny with medaka.

Chromosome 6 is identified as the dot chromosome based on its significantly smaller size compared to the other chromosomes.

Assembly: The assembly ioAnaJuni1.hap2.1 is based on 47x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. A large number of centromeric repeats is in the unlocalised sequences. A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 1 (116.0-118.4 Mbp). The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 5 (87.0 - 97.5 Mbp) and chromosome 6 (0.0 - 4.0 Mbp) are unknown. The X chromosome was identified by read coverage, no Y chromosome was found.

Assembly: The assembly icSphBipu4.1 is based on 51x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Sex chromosomes were assigned based on the copy number on the diploid HiC map. PacBio and HiC came from different samples, so some variations and lack of HiC signal could be observed. Haplotypic inversion detected on Chromosome 11 from 1.45 to 5.86 Mbp.

Description: Alces alces genome assembly, chromosome: 6
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Mammalia; Eutheria; Laurasiatheria; Artiodactyla; Ruminantia; Pecora; Cervidae; Odocoileinae; Alces
Assembly: This project presents the diploid genome assembly of the Eurasian elk, Alces alces, with a sample provided by Kristian Sather. The data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The mAlcAlc1.1 assembly is based on 22x PacBio HiFi and 73x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt and assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies, ensuring that reads for hap1 scaffolding did not contain k-mers unique to hap2, and vice versa. YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes, as confirmed by Hi-C data, are named according to size. Both sex chromosomes have been included in hap1. Contigs with a coverage below 10x were removed from both haplotype assemblies.

Assembly: The assembly csSphLind2.1 is based on 52x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome 22 is a microchromosome. Based on the PacBio read coverage it could be overcollapsed and exist in more than one copy. Chromosome 21 is about 1/4th of the size of an autosome, which is similar to what Healey, A.L., et. al (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41477-022-01333-5) observed for potential U/V sex chromosomes in other Sphagnums. Chromosome 20 seems to be made up of very repetitive sequence.

Assembly: The assembly ilLamCult1.hap1.1 is based on 52x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements~(https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).

Assembly: The assembly ddDesSoph1.hap1.1 is based on 90x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome appears to be a degenerated tetraploid. As a result each pf the chromosomes has HiC signal that suggests it is an old copy of another. For example Chromsomes 1 and 2.

Assembly: The assembly mMyoMys1.hap2.1 is based on 46x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosomes X and Y were assigned based on read coverage and HIC data.

Assembly: The assembly icLorLunu1.1 is based on 29x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The X chromosome was identified based on synteny with Philonthus spinipes (GCA_963082785.1). The order and orientation of contigs in the following regions is uncertain: chromosome 2, 32.5 Mb to 36 Mb; chromosome 3, 27 Mb to 34 Mb; chromosome 4; chromosome 5, 27.8 Mb to 37.5 Mb; chromosome 6, 24.5 Mb to 34.5 Mb, 25.5 Mb to 40 Mb; chromosome 7, 25 Mb to 33 Mb; chromosome 8, 22 Mb to 32 Mb; chromosome 9, 23.7 Mb to 30 Mb; chromosome X, 25 Mb to 39 Mb.

Assembly: The assembly qqMetBour1.1 is based on 25x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. Assembly: The assembly mPipPip1.1 is based on 62x PacBio data, 34x 10X Genomics Chromium data, and 58x Arima Hi-C data generated at the Wellcome Sanger Institute. The Hi-C data was derived from a separate sample, mPipPip2. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Falcon-unzip, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, 10X based scaffolding with scaff10x, Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2, Arrow polishing, two rounds of FreeBayes polishing. The mitochondrial assembly was produced at The Rockefeller University using mitoVGP. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ilPerHydr1.hap2.1 is based on 153x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly. The order and orientation of scaffolds making up the W chromosomes is uncertain. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 3 (13.0 - 13.9 Mbp) are unknown.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Mammalia; Eutheria; Laurasiatheria; Artiodactyla; Ruminantia; Pecora; Cervidae; Odocoileinae; Alces
Assembly: This project presents the alternate haplotype genome assembly of the Eurasian elk, Alces alces, with a sample provided by Kristian Sather. The data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The mAlcAlc1.1 assembly is based on 22x PacBio HiFi and 73x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt and assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies, ensuring that reads for hap1 scaffolding did not contain k-mers unique to hap2, and vice versa. YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes, as confirmed by Hi-C data, are named according to size. Both sex chromosomes have been included in hap1. Contigs with a coverage below 10x were removed from both haplotype assemblies.

Assembly: The assembly mLutLut1.2 is based on 63x PacBio data, 58x 10X Genomics Chromium data, BioNano data and 17x Hi-C data generated at the Baylor College of Medicine. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Falcon-unzip, retained haplotig identification with Purge Haplotigs, 10X based scaffolding with scaff10x, Hi-C based scaffolding and improvement by the Aiden lab as part of a collaboration with DNA Zoo using 3D-DNA and Juicebox, Arrow polishing, and two rounds of FreeBayes polishing. The mitochondrial assembly was produced at The Rockefeller University using mitoVGP. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ilAbrSylv1.hap1.1 is based on 45x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Assembly: The assembly mCerEla1.hap2.2 is based on 30x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds are named by synteny based on Cervus elaphus (red deer) GCA_910594005.1. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 17 (14.5-17.0 Mbp) is unknown. The chromosomes were named and oriented based on Cervus elaphus (red deer) GCA_910594005.1.
